draft.introduction: Amos 5:21–24 contains one of the most powerful prophetic denunciations of religious ritual divorced from social justice. Martin Luther King Jr. quoted its climactic verse—“let justice roll down like waters”—in his most famous speeches.

translation.text: I hate, I despise your feasts, and I take no delight in your solemn assemblies. Even though you offer me your burnt offerings and grain offerings, I will not accept them. Take away from me the noise of your songs; to the melody of your harps I will not listen. But let justice roll down like waters, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.

translator_notes.note: Amos, a shepherd from Tekoa, was the first of the writing prophets and established the principle that worship without justice is an abomination to God. This verse became the theological foundation of liberation theology and the civil rights movement.
